channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP's immigration pitch to New Jersey Latinos aims for some distance from Trump in governor’s race
item.description: Jack Ciattarelli has been endorsed by Donald Trump in next month's New Jersey governor's election and is a strong supporter of the Republican president and his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown weighs heavy on the US labor market
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping crackdown on immigration is throwing foreigners out of work and shaking the American economy and job market.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman And Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sherrill pulls out all stops with Obama endorsement, star-studded New Jersey campaign push as race tightens
item.description: Rep. Mikie Sherrill, D-N.J., campaigns with potential 2028 presidential candidates Govs. Gretchen Whitmer and Wes Moore in final push in New Jersey gubernatorial race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the new 'Squad': The next generation of Trump-era progressive congressional candidates
item.description: A new wave of progressive candidates, including Saikat Chakrabarti and Dr. Abdul El-Sayed, are seeking congressional seats amid President Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Diegans host rally for Ukraine following President Trump's meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: President Donald Trump met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House today to discuss ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Dominique LaVigne, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump drops the F-bomb before the cameras. It wasn't the first time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump talks plainly, bluntly, often even coarsely. But it’s rare he drops an actual F-bomb as cameras are rolling.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolton pleads not guilty in Justice Department case accusing him of sharing government secrets
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — John Bolton pleaded not guilty Friday to charges accusing the former Trump national security adviser turned critic of emailing classified information to family members and keeping top secret documents at his Maryland home.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow National Guard in Chicago
item.description: Trump is requesting the Supreme Court toss out an order by a lower court judge that prevented the Guard from coming to the city for at least two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Obama continues tradition of slow-rolled political endorsements as gubernatorial elections hit fever pitch
item.description: Former President Barack Obama officially backed both Democrats running in this year's only gubernatorial elections in New Jersey and Virginia after staying mum on the races.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP turns Schumer print quotes into AI-generated video of him saying shutdown good for Democrats
item.description: Senate Republican campaign leaders are using artificial intelligence-generated video to falsely depict Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer appearing to say on camera comments he made in a print publication interview about the political impacts of the partial government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Russia proposes 'Putin-Trump Tunnel' with Elon Musk's help to connect nations under Bering Strait
item.description: President Vladimir Putin's investment envoy outlined a plan for U.S.-Russia tunnel connecting Alaska and Siberia through Bering Strait within eight years at $8 billion cost.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: St. Paul mayoral candidates lay out their visions for Minnesota's capital city
item.description: Three candidates for St. Paul mayor discuss key issues in a MPR News and Pioneer Press debate. Incumbent Melvin Carter, state Rep. Kaohly Her and scientist Yan Chen address city spending, taxes, amenities and public safety.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Jeff Jones, MPR News

item.title: Democrats say Trump needs to be involved in shutdown talks. He's shown little interest in doing so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is showing little urgency to broker a compromise that would end the government shutdown, even as Democrats insist no breakthrough is possible without his direct involvement. Three weeks in, Congress is at a standstill.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ahead of Zelenskyy meeting, Trump shows signs he might not be ready to send Kyiv Tomahawk missiles
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to host Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for talks at the White House on Friday. The U.S. leader is signaling he’s not ready to agree to sell Kyiv a long-range missile system that the Ukrainians say they desperately need.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Court rejects Trump administration bid to deploy troops in Chicago amid appeal
item.description: A federal appeals court on Thursday rejected the Trump administration's request to lift an order preventing the deployment of the National Guard across Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Bolton surrenders at Maryland courthouse
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — John Bolton, the former national security adviser-turned-foe of President Trump, surrendered himself to authorities Friday morning after he was charged with unlawfully sharing and storing classified information. Bolton arrived at the federal courthouse in Greenbelt, Md.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the New York City mayor's debate
item.description: It was the first time the candidates were back on the debate stage since Mayor Eric Adams exited the race in September.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump expresses optimism for Ukraine deal as Zelenskyy arrives in DC
item.description: “We need Tomahawks for the United States of America too,” Trump said. “We have a lot of them, but we need them. I mean, we can’t deplete our country.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hey, ‘View,’ here are some Republicans who’d love to be guests
item.description: Conservative pundits and lawmakers pushed back on "The View" co-host comments that no GOP members will come on the show.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘We roundly condemn’ Young Republicans group chat, swastika American flag
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Thursday that Republicans “roundly condemn” the group chat among Young Republican officials that contained racist, antisemitic and homophobic comments, as well as an American flag with a swastika that was pictured behind an aide in a GOP congressman’s office.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Government shutdown threatens food, family aid
item.description: If the government shutdown continues into November, federal officials have told states that funding for SNAP benefits will run out. That means more than 440,000 Minnesotans might not receive federal food aid and will struggle even more to feed their families.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate: Teachers to pledge academic oath
item.description: A new bill just filed at the statehouse would make Florida educators take an oath to uphold the constitution and state laws before ever stepping foot into a classroom. It's similar to what lawyers, doctors, and public officials pledge.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Gateway project status could impact NJ governor race
item.description: SECAUCUS, N.J. (PIX11) — The drama and uncertainty surrounding the Gateway Tunnel project has become the latest wrinkle in the race for New Jersey governor, just a couple of weeks out from the election.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Make California an independent country? Fresno man's ballot initiative moves forward
item.description: Marcus Evans, a Fresno man, has moved one step closer to making California an independent country, as the California Secretary of State's Office has announced that the initiative can now start collecting petition signatures.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, Fox 5

item.title: At least 27 states turned over sensitive data about food stamp recipients to USDA
item.description: Democratic-led states secured a legal victory to keep the personal data of food recipients out of the federal government's reach. But NPR's reporting shows that millions of records on Americans have already been shared.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: Tim Walz, Tom Emmer feud ratchets up
item.description: Minnesota’s top Democrat and top Republican take frequent rhetorical jabs at one another although they’re not running against one another. The spat could have negative consequences for the people they jointly represent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Government shutdown brings back memories for Minnesotan at center of 1995 shutdown
item.description: Annette Meeks, who was deputy chief of staff for Republican U.S. Rep. Newt Gingrich during the 21-day shutdown in 1995, spoke about government shutdowns with MPR News guest host Emily Bright on Morning Edition.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: In rural America, scarce doctors battle misinformation as they practice medicine
item.description: Conspiracy theories about health fill a vacuum created by the lack of doctors in many rural communities. Meanwhile, doctors in these areas say patients have become increasingly distrustful and sometimes hostile.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi, MPR News

item.title: Grand jury proceedings and subpoenas surround Hope Florida
item.description: Hope Florida, a program aiming to help folks get off government assistance, has faced scrutiny this past year over a mysterious money trail that started with a Medicaid settlement and ended with a political action committee.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
